fr.recommendations: Enhancing responder effectiveness could be achieved by managing dense vegetation around the perimeter and in key travel corridors to ensure clear visibility and unobstructed access paths. Consider trimming or strategically thinning the landscaped areas to facilitate improved sight lines and ease the movement of emergency personnel. The addition of clearly marked and wide access points through the perimeter fencing would assist in smoother navigation, staging, and entry. Ensuring open green spaces are maintained free of unnecessary obstructions could provide adequate staging areas for emergency operations, facilitating a more efficient and coordinated response in the event of an emergency situation.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the defensible space around the property could be achieved by selectively thinning the dense tree canopy and reducing vegetation clusters, especially near the structure, to diminish fuel continuity. Prioritizing the removal or trimming of vegetation adjacent to the structure can contribute to structure exposure reduction. Maintaining a clear buffer along the vegetated perimeter and ensuring separation of fuels around the home will further enhance defensible space. Along the vegetated access route, managing overgrown vegetation can improve firefighter access and support suppression efforts. Utilizing the river as a natural fuel break while preserving open areas adjacent to it could aid in reducing opportunities for fire spread, supporting long-term wildfire resilience and property preparedness. Regularly managing and monitoring vegetation growth throughout the property will foster a sustainable balance between fuel reduction and natural beauty, supporting ongoing wildfire readiness.
